mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents using standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for new users.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti CLI tool, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> Use this command to download and install the CLI tool.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.
commands: gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
This step installs Kagenti on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Install Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaler configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstall the Kagenti Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade the Kagenti installation.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating: pods "kagenti-" is forbidden: error looking up service account kagenti/kagenti: serviceaccount "kagenti" not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account for Kagenti was not created during installation.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl create serviceaccount kagenti -n kagenti' to create the service account.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "kagenti/kagenti:0.5.1": rpc error: code = NotFound desc = manifest for kagenti/kagenti:0.5.1 not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version does not exist in the container registry.

**Fix:**
Check the available versions and update the Helm chart with a valid image version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster creation command and check the Google Cloud Console for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kagenti pods are in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
